heading: Why Order Matters
body: **Lexicographic ordering** is a fixed pattern for filling the base-variable columns of a truth table. It guarantees that every possible truth-value assignment appears exactly once.

examples.text: For $P$, $Q$, and $R$, the table must include all 8 possible combinations of T and F.
Making up values row by row can miss a combination or repeat one.

heading: Left Column First
body: The **leftmost variable** changes slowest. In an 8-row table, its values come in one block of 4 T values, then one block of 4 F values.

examples.text: For $P$, use `T T T T F F F F`.
For $P$, use `T F T F T F T F`.

note: This rule assumes $P$ is the leftmost base variable.

heading: Halve The Block
body: Each new variable to the right changes twice as fast as the one before it. Its block size is half the previous block size.

examples.text: If $P$ uses blocks of 4, then $Q$ uses blocks of 2: `T T F F T T F F`.
If $P$ uses blocks of 4, then $Q$ should also use blocks of 4.

heading: Right Column Fastest
body: The **rightmost variable** changes every row. In an 8-row table, it alternates T and F from top to bottom.

examples.text: For $R$, use `T F T F T F T F`.
For $R$, use `T T F F T T F F`.

heading: Three Variable Setup
body: For a truth table with $P$, $Q$, and $R$, fill the base columns before evaluating any compound parts.

examples.text: $P$: `T T T T F F F F`
$Q$: `T T F F T T F F`
$R$: `T F T F T F T F`

note: This pattern lists all 8 combinations once each.

heading: Do Not Mix Speeds
body: A common mistake is giving two variables the same switching pattern. Each column must have its own rate of change, from slowest on the left to fastest on the right.

examples.text: If both $Q$ and $R$ are `T F T F T F T F`, the table is wrong.
If $P$, $Q$, and $R$ all alternate every row, the table still covers every combination.

heading: Core Pattern
body: Fill base-variable columns from left to right with blocks that keep halving. The leftmost variable changes slowest, and the rightmost changes every row.
